pr.title: Fix sampling not starting: eliminate double likelihood eval and fix kwargs passing
pr.body:
NUTS sampling hangs on startup when using `gpu_logL(use_pmap=True)` with numpyro because the model evaluates the likelihood twice per step through `pure_callback`, doubling Python↔XLA round-trips.

## Changes

- **Eliminate redundant likelihood call** — `numpyro_model()` called `logx(pars)` which internally recomputes `logL(pars)` (already computed for the deterministic). Replaced with `logl + logx.logprior(pars)` to reuse the result:

```python
# Before: evaluates likelihood twice per step
logl = logx.logL(pars)
numpyro.deterministic('log_likelihood', logl)
numpyro.factor('logl', logx(pars))  # logx() calls logL() again internally

# After: single evaluation
logl = logx.logL(pars)
numpyro.deterministic('log_likelihood', logl)
numpyro.factor('logl', logl + logx.logprior(pars))
```

- **Fix broken kwargs forwarding in `makesampler_nuts`** — Dict comprehensions used `for arg in kwargs.items()` with undefined `val`. Fixed tuple unpacking and kwargs now properly override defaults:

```python
# Before: NameError on any kwarg usage
**{arg: val for arg in kwargs.items() if arg in ...}

# After:
nuts_valid = {arg: val for arg, val in kwargs.items() if arg in ...}
nutsargs = {**nuts_defaults, **nuts_valid}
```
pr.author: Copilot
